purpose: Record pre-design facts for the HTTPS proxy benchmark trust repair controlled run.

observations.statement: The current benchmark fixture is a Python HTTPS proxy that sends HTTP proxy responses with ssl.SSLSocket.sendall and records proxy_response_send_us around that call.

The checked-in gen005 S8 final benchmark contains a 0.890x throughput ratio for HTTP over HTTPS proxy at 1000 requests, while the same cell has zero errors and identical proxy connection/request/body counts for ylong_http_client_sync and libcurl.

Local analysis found gen005 elapsed time is strongly correlated with proxy_response_send_us in the outlier cell: about 0.999 for ylong_http_client_sync and about 0.990 for libcurl; paired throughput ratio is negatively correlated with proxy send ratio.
evidence: interactive analysis command output in current requirements session

observations.statement: A targeted 20-repeat rerun of HTTP over HTTPS proxy at 1000 requests did not reproduce the 0.890x result: ratio-of-mean throughput was about 1.267x, paired geomean about 1.242x, with ylong winning 14 of 20 paired repeats.

A reverse-order 20-repeat rerun, libcurl before ylong in each repeat, still produced ylong advantage: ratio-of-mean throughput about 1.307x, paired geomean about 1.296x, with ylong winning 14 of 20 paired repeats.
The figure currently uses ratio-of-mean throughput for each scenario/request cell, which can diverge from paired elapsed or paired geomean when one client has low-latency outliers.

limits: This source context records pre-design facts only. It does not choose the final fixture design.
Temporary /tmp rerun artifacts are diagnostic evidence and must be replaced by run-local persisted evidence during runtime before final claims.
